module_mood_contract: M1 joyful creation — the bug fix itself

rules_evaluated.evidence: Three registry-expanded prompts (fix-tests-first-1..3). Tests-first + interrogate + TDD-on-surface all forcing functions in prompts. Commit/push/PR is intentionally prose-led per maintainer note line 45.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reading burden for verification — Claude audits Claude.
rules_evaluated.evidence: fix-tests-first-2 ('interrogate the fix, name what's still surface') is Claude-on-Claude verification, not student manually diffing. Push-back-on-the-line is student bringing voice-of-experience.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Open-hook for student input — bug after the colon.
rules_evaluated.evidence: fix-tests-first-1 takes 'Drop your bug after the colon' — open-hook pattern; bug is concrete to student.
fix-tests-first-2 verb: 'interrogate'. Body verbs: read, push back, ask, name. No defend / justify shapes.
Three markers each with action lead-in.
Strategy doc M1 mood: joyful creation. PR shipping moment lands the mood.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategy-fidelity — no plan mode at M1; tests-first.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body line 13: 'No plan mode here. Plan mode earns its keep in M2 on multi-file work; on a trivial bug, it's overhead.' Strategy compliant.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — compound is Ex3 territory.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body line 9: 'The compound move (writing down what you learned) lives in the next exercise.'
Body line 20: 'If a line isn't what you'd have written, push back. Quote the line and say why. Whoever has the better argument wins.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity to strategy M1 Ex2.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body matches strategy: tests-first, root-cause-driven, real PR ships.
